meta.title: Casale La Cocca · Exclusive private villa · Itria Valley, Puglia
meta.description: A 1950s stone farmhouse in the Itria Valley, entirely yours. Five en-suite suites, saltwater infinity pool, exclusive use. Between Cisternino and Ostuni, in the heart of authentic Puglia.

hero.title: Casale La Cocca
hero.subtitle: 1950s stone, centuries-old olive trees, a pool that dissolves into the horizon. Entirely yours.
hero.location: In the countryside between Cisternino, Ostuni and Martina Franca
hero.imageAlt: The infinity pool at Casale La Cocca at dusk, with the stone farmhouse beyond

overview.title: Where time moves differently
overview.body: A farmhouse from the 1950s, returned to its essence by local craftsmen. Limestone vaults, traditional tiled floors, thick walls that hold the summer cool. Outside, fourteen metres of saltwater that disappear into the landscape. Inside, five suites with private bathrooms and rooms that ask only one thing: that you stay.

pullquote.text: True luxury makes no noise. It makes light.

gallery.title: Stone, light, water
gallery.subtitle: Author photography. Every image is a time of day that never repeats itself.

spaces.title: Four ways to live the villa
spaces.subtitle: The main house and three independent suites. Each with its own character, each with its own en-suite bathroom. No shared corridors, no neighbouring rooms.

casale.name: Main house
casale.body: The heart of the villa. Limestone vaults, a living room with fireplace, an open kitchen framed by the original arches. Two master en-suite suites. The sitting room opens onto the pool and garden without interruption: inside and outside become the same place.

poolSuite.name: Pool Suite
poolSuite.body: Waking up with water in front of your eyes. The glass doors open onto the pool and garden: the distance from bed to water is counted in steps, not minutes. En-suite bathroom.

gardenSuite.name: Garden Suite
gardenSuite.body: Glass doors onto the garden, light filtered through olive trees, air that smells of warm stone. A ground-floor suite with direct access to the outdoors. Puglia comes into the room. En-suite bathroom.

firstFloorSuite.name: First-floor suite
firstFloorSuite.body: A private external staircase, a terrace entirely your own. From above: the silhouette of the olive trees, the blue of the pool, the silence. En-suite bathroom. You only find this place if you want to be found.

outdoors.title: Water, olive trees, sky
outdoors.body: The 14×4-metre saltwater infinity pool has no edge, it has a horizon. Around it: a panoramic sun deck, an outdoor kitchen equipped for dinners that last into the night, a gas barbecue, an outdoor shower. And centuries-old olive trees that give shade without being asked.

amenities.title: Nothing missing. Nothing intrudes.
amenities.subtitle: Every detail has been chosen so that the only thing on your mind is the quality of the time passing.
amenities.note: Video surveillance is switched off during your stay.

location.title: Three hill towns. A walking path. The sea in 20 minutes.
location.body: In the heart of the Itria Valley, 400 metres from the Apulian Aqueduct path. Cisternino, Locorotondo, Martina Franca within 10 km. Ostuni in 20 minutes. The sea at Torre Canne in the same. Removed from the world, but never far from it.
location.mapAlt: Map of Casale La Cocca's location in the Itria Valley, between Cisternino and Ostuni

t1.text: "A week suspended in time. The infinity pool, the stone, the silence, everything speaks of a luxury that doesn't shout."
t1.author: Family M., Milan · summer 2024

t2.text: "We celebrated a milestone birthday here. The outdoor kitchen, the barbecue, the pool at sunset: it was perfect."
t2.author: Group of friends, Rome · July 2024

t3.text: "The location is incredible: total quiet, but Martina Franca is ten minutes away. We'll be back."
t3.author: Couple, London · September 2024

testimonials.disclaimer: Reviews collected from booking platforms. Names have been abbreviated for privacy.

footer.tagline: Stone, olive trees, water. Itria Valley, Puglia.
